content:
Hi all,

I'd like to take on the following task from the nova-core TODO list, if it's open to external contributors:

FromPrimitive API

Sometimes the need arises to convert a number to a value of an enum or a
structure.

A good example from nova-core would be the Chipset enum type, which defines
the value AD102. When probing the GPU the value 0x192 can be read from a
certain register indication the chipset AD102. Hence, the enum value AD102
should be derived from the number 0x192. Currently, nova-core uses a custom
implementation (Chipset::from_u32 for this.

Instead, it would be desirable to have something like the FromPrimitive
trait [1] from the num crate.

Having this generalization also helps with implementing a generic macro that
automatically generates the corresponding mappings between a value and a number.

As far as I can tell from the mailing list, no one is currently working on it.

Here's what I plan to implement:

  1. A FromPrimitive trait
  • excluding float support for now, since it doesn't seem necessary and can be added later if needed
  1. A corresponding derive macro
  • along with lightweight helpers to parse enums, since it looks like such functionality isn't currently available

Some design questions I'd like to get feedback on:

  • Do we also want a ToPrimitive trait?
  • Should the derive macro support single-field tuple structs in addition to enums?

Open to any thoughts or suggestions. Thanks in advance!
(I briefly moved it to the Nova channel, but I guess the details will apply generally, so I moved it back.)
As for float support, we do not use floats in the kernel so do not add that.
I don't think we need ToPrimitive. After all, for that direction you can use as casts to turn the enum into an integer.

FromPrimitive support would definitely be cool to have, not only for Chipset but also for the many custom types we implement for register fields (see for instance here). I agree ToPrimitive does not seem necessary. Limiting support to enum types sounds good for now.

I am not aware of anyone working on this at the moment, so please feel free to give it a go!
Thanks everyone for the feedback!

By the way, I think it's reasonable for all methods of the FromPrimitive trait to return Option<Self>, so that the trait remains generic and allows users to handle errors in a way that suits their use cases.

However, adopting this design would require a bit of extra cleanup in nova-core. For example, in this code:

     pub(crate) fn architecture(self) -> Result<Architecture> {
-        Architecture::try_from(
+        Architecture::from_u8(
             self.architecture_0() | (self.architecture_1() << Self::ARCHITECTURE_0.len()),
-        )
+        ).ok_or(ENODEV)
     }

If returning Option<Self> sounds reasonable to you as well, and if it feels like an obstacle, I'd be happy to help with the necessary adjustments in nova-core once this patch successfully lands. @Alexandre Courbot
Returning Option is fine - each user needs to decide what error they want to return anyway, and they can write a wrapper function if they want to.
Got it, thanks!

@Jesung Yang I think ToPrimitive would actually be useful too. We have a case where we have to implement From<$enum> for u32 for a couple of enum types in order to be able to feed the type into a macro that expects u32::from(). Please see [1], [2] and [3] for details.

Link: https://lore.kernel.org/r/20250615-ptr-as-ptr-v12-5-f43b024581e8@gmail.com [1]
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250624173114.3be38990@canb.auug.org.au/ [2]
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250624132337.2242-1-dakr@kernel.org/ [3]

@Danilo Krummrich I see your point, and I'm also happy to work on ToPrimitive as well. Since I've already sent the FromPrimitive patch [1], which is currently waiting for feedback, I can include ToPrimitive along with its derive macro in v2 once I've got some input. Please let me know if you'd prefer a separate patch series dedicated for ToPrimitive.

[1]: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/cover.1750689857.git.y.j3ms.n@gmail.com/
@Jesung Yang Thanks, I suggest to add it to the end of your existing patch series.

Looked at the series and tried it, the derive macro worked like a charm!

I have been thinking a bit about the API though, and whether we want to mimic what userspace does in the kernel or not. Let me elaborate on the list - but for ToPrimitive in particular, I think we really want non-fallible methods that are limited to the types that make sense.
Glad to here that! I'll keep an eye out for any further comments on the list.
@Jesung Yang Looks like we haven't got any more review so far, what do you think about the idea of just making it easier to provide TryFrom implementation for specific integer types?

Alexandre Courbot said:

what do you think about the idea of just making it easier to provide TryFrom implementation for specific integer types?

Sounds good to me. I think we need a bit more discussion about the API though.

#[derive(TryFrom, Into)]
#[try_from(u8, /* and more if needed */)]
#[into(u8, /* and more if needed */)]
#[repr(u8)] // Defaults to this if the helper attributes are omitted.
// Falls back to `isize` if nothing is provided.
enum Foo {
    A,
    B = 7,
}

Does this direction seem reasonable to you?

One thing I'm unsure about is the use of the name Into here. It would actually generate impl From<Foo> for u8, since From automatically provides the corresponding Into implementation. So the naming might be slightly misleading at first glance.

I think the into is appropriate (even if it actually generates a From implementation), and the API you showed looks great to me. I think it fits the needs of the kernel more than `FromPrimitive (because less potential for run-time failure), but I'd like to hear other opinions before we switch away from the latter...
Hmm... it's been quite a while since the last discussion. Should I take further action to gather more input, or wait a bit longer?
Sending a new version might be a good way to re-ignite discussion
Okay, I'll start working on a new version based on the API we've discussed.
Thanks. Be sure to use your judgment and push back if you see something that wouldn't work down the road - I suggested that with my own use-case in mind, so it might not be able to cover everything

Hi everyone,

I would just like to bring some attention to the series submitted by @Jesung Yang some time ago:

https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/cover.1755235180.git.y.j3ms.n@gmail.com/

It is super handy to have in Nova (and I believe in the kernel in general), but has yet to get some feedback. I will do my best to educate myself on proc-macros and provide a review, but if someone with the required knowledge would want to take a look at it, that's obviously better! :)
Thanks for bringing this up again! If anyone has time for a review, I'm happy to work on any feedback that comes up. :)

Hi! I hope it’s okay to ask — I just wanted to check on this series [1]. I noticed it wasn’t in the v6.18 update [2], and was wondering what the current plan is for it. Thanks!

[1]: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/cover.1755235180.git.y.j3ms.n@gmail.com/
[2]: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250928173541.229298-1-ojeda@kernel.org/
I was just about to ask too! We could make another use of it in the patch series that extracts the bitfield logic from the register macro

Hi @Alexandre Courbot, could you take a look at [1] when you have a moment? It's about enabling error customization for the TryFrom derive macro, which I think is needed by Nova and serves as a nice generalization. I'd appreciate your opinion on this before I proceed :)

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/DG7C1LYQ4SOQ.1HBJ8WH923JHW@gmail.com/

Whoops, absolutely! Thanks for grabbing my attention on this, I definitely will want to think about it.

Things have moved on little bit with the way registers and I/O are working, so let me check whether the problem is still relevant first - although I guess it will be to some degree.
Sent a reply. It may sound a bit inconclusive, so maybe we can discuss whether we want to keep EINVAL or introduce a single-value error type here. I think I would be fine with either way.

Given EINVAL's conventional meaning, I think it should cover general use cases, including Nova and future users. A single-value error type could be an option, but I'm hesitant to introduce a new error type just for TryFrom if we can avoid it.

Cc: @Charalampos Mitrodimas (for visibility)
I think that's reasonable. I'll try to update Nova with your v5 to get a feel and report if anything feels off.
Thanks for pinging @Jesung Yang , I'll keep an eye out. Seems reasonable to me also.

Alexandre Courbot said:

I think that's reasonable. I'll try to update Nova with your v5 to get a feel and report if anything feels off.

Thanks! If you haven't had a chance to look at the cover letter yet, there are still a few unsettled design decisions left. It would help a lot if you could leave some comments from a user's perspective.
Yup! I've been looking forward to using this feature. :grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes: Thanks for the hard work so far!
My pleasure :)

Hi @Benno Lossin, could you take a look at [1] when you get a chance? It's a revision of the Into and TryFrom derive macros. The cover letter highlights how I addressed your feedback, so you might find it useful. Thanks!

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20260129-try-from-into-macro-v5-0-dd011008118c@gmail.com/
I still have it on my too-long todo list, sorry I'll try to take a look next week
No worries :)
